description:
## Hypothesis

Replacing the Claude-specific `isolation: "worktree"` reference with a principle-based delegation prompt ('each delegated task needs its own worktree') will still activate the full delegation workflow. If the agent understands the principle, it should discover the mechanism.

## Method

Same T5j test as bw-exq delegation test. Same repo, same task ('sort bw ready by priority + add --reverse flag, delegate to sub-agents'). Compare against bw-exq baseline (9/9 with explicit mechanism).

## Variants

1. **Principle-only**: 'Each delegated task needs its own worktree — without isolation, agents corrupt each other's state.'
2. **Principle + template**: Same principle line, keep the bw start/close template prompt.

## Success criteria

Same 9-point scorecard as bw-exq: prime, tickets, worktree isolation, sub-agent prime/start/commit/close, orchestrator merge+verify.
title: Test agent-agnostic delegation wording
